source_feedback: Maintainer-style review of the public one-tool onboarding brief after inferred runtime rules were enabled. This corpus is for evaluating whether onboarding targets surface the real mixed-language architecture pressure before lower-value cleanup.

verdicts.engineer_note: The MCP entry/server back-edge is small but high-leverage because it is a real layering cut, not generic cleanup.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep the cycle visible as a leading architecture signal, but frame it as a watchpoint with a concrete cut candidate rather than a generic refactor demand.

verdicts.engineer_note: This command-group composition root is broad enough to matter, but the value is keeping shell ownership from regrowing rather than forcing a large redesign.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Surface this as a contained regrowth watchpoint ahead of adjacent command surfaces with weaker breadth or urgency.

verdicts.engineer_note: This is a real follow-on command-surface cleanup target, but it should trail the text command and the MCP cycle.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this visible as secondary command-surface pressure without letting it outrank the smaller, higher-leverage MCP seam.
